title: Memory Remove Current State Feature
goal: Implement #455 by removing current-memory as a shared current-state feature while preserving durable memory and migration guidance.
non_goals: Do not remove planning current-state ownership.
Do not preserve stale current-memory through compatibility layers indefinitely.

intent.outcome: New installs do not create shared memory current-state files, existing installs get migration/shrink/delete pressure, and planning remains the shared active-state owner when installed.
intent.constraints: Keep Memory focused on durable anti-rediscovery knowledge and keep local-only scratch optional/non-authoritative.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest package changes and tests needed to remove current-memory as a normal shared feature.
intent.escalation: Escalate if removal would break durable Memory or require broad compatibility guarantees for stale current state.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, close #455, refresh external evidence, then promote #456.
execution.proof: memory package tests, workspace CLI tests as needed, ruff, proof selector, planning doctor

invariants: Durable Memory remains installed and useful.
Planning owns shared active/current state when installed.
Local-only scratch remains optional and non-authoritative.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #450: make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #456-#460 plus #467/#469/#471 remain in the feature-tier and surface-merge lane.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #455 closeout, promote the next #450 child indicated by validation

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Memory no longer installs shared project/task current-state files, and existing files are migration pressure instead of normal shared state.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Advanced feature opt-in classification remains in #456.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Docs, skills, README, package payloads, and tests all needed the same wording boundary so current-memory did not survive as implied doctrine.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: memory tests, workspace CLI tests, ruff, maintainer surfaces, proof selector, root CLI authority defaults, and workspace doctor passed.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for #455; broader #450 continuation remains open.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Remove current-memory as a shared current-state feature.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Do not remove durable Memory or planning active-state ownership.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Whether current-memory files should be removed from payloads, flagged for migration, or both.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Compatibility would require keeping current-memory as an active shared surface.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Remove a competing current-state surface instead of making it more capable.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this make Memory smaller and planning/current-state ownership clearer?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, close #455, refresh external evidence, then promote #456.
invariants: Durable Memory remains installed and useful.
Planning owns shared active/current state when installed.
Local-only scratch remains optional and non-authoritative.
validation_commands: rg -n "project-state|task-context|bootstrap-populate|current-memory|repo/current" .agentic-workspace/memory packages/memory src tests docs README.md
uv run ruff check src tests packages/memory/src packages/memory/tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/memory-remove-current-state-feature.plan.json packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap/installer.py packages/memory/tests/test_installer.py packages/memory/bootstrap/.agentic-workspace/memory src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning,memory --format json
completion_criteria: New memory installs do not create shared current-memory current/task files.
Existing current-memory files are flagged for migration, shrink, or deletion.
Planning remains the only shared active-state owner when installed.
Memory docs no longer describe current-memory as a normal shared feature.
Tests cover install, doctor, or report behavior without current-memory.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Removed project-state/task-context current-memory files from the shipped payload, package mirror, and installed root memory; removed bootstrap-populate; updated installer current checks to flag legacy current-memory as migration residue; updated docs/skills/README guidance so Memory does not own shared active state.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: uv run pytest packages/memory/tests -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; rg stale-reference sweep; uv run ruff check src tests packages/memory/src packages/memory/tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning,memory --format json.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: The durable product boundary was captured in Memory package docs, bootstrap payload docs, installed memory workflow/skills, and tests.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Docs now say current-memory is optional routing calibration and legacy migration residue; active state belongs to planning/status or local-only scratch.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest packages/memory/tests -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; rg stale-reference sweep; uv run ruff check src tests packages/memory/src packages/memory/tests packages/planning/src packages/planning/tests; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning,memory --format json.
proof_report.proof achieved now: New payloads install only routing-feedback under current/, legacy project-state/task-context files are migration-review pressure, docs no longer describe current-memory as normal shared state, and tests cover package/install/report behavior.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: The package payload, installed root memory, docs, skills, installer checks, and tests were updated together and the selected validation lanes passed.

discard.summary: Per-file migration mechanics and temporary assertion failures are not durable knowledge.

continuation.summary: Promote #456 to keep shrinking ordinary startup and classifying advanced surfaces as opt-in.

memory.summary: Docs now say current-memory is optional routing calibration and legacy migration residue; active state belongs to planning/status or local-only scratch.

config_check.summary: Docs now say current-memory is optional routing calibration and legacy migration residue; active state belongs to planning/status or local-only scratch.

docs.summary: Memory docs and skills now make planning/status the active-state owner and treat current-memory project/task files as migration residue.
Docs now say current-memory is optional routing calibration and legacy migration residue; active state belongs to planning/status or local-only scratch.
